mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid drop in the unemployment rate below 7.0 percent signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending expectations, driving immediate positive equity returns as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.0 percent over a 5-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real residential investment (HOUST) and consumer credit balances (RSAFS) confirms broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 55 percent of out-of-sample rolling windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 100 on the 2017 base scale indicates supply chain normalization that expands profit margins for cyclical equities, yielding positive forward returns specifically when inflation remains contained.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO breaks above 100 during periods where CPIAUCSL annualized inflation exceeds 3.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently exceeding 2.5 percent annualized rate sustains corporate earnings momentum, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations across all market cycles.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference between high-GDP and low-GDP subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings surpassing 3.0 percent annualized inflation trigger immediate equity sell-offs as markets price in aggressive central bank tightening and compressed real yields.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances following CPIAUCSL inflation spikes above 3.0 percent over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth exceeding 200,000 monthly and unemployment falling below 7.5 percent validates a self-reinforcing labor market recovery that accelerates consumer spending and lifts equity earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative or flat 20-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered periods, or the interaction term fails to improve model R-squared by at least 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts consistently above 900 thousand units combined with GDP growth above 2.0 percent signals a durable housing-led recovery that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when HOUST crosses above 900 but GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5 percent annualized, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 3-year rolling window.
